front: What are Iceberg Tables in Snowflake, and how does Snowflake's Iceberg integration work?
back: Snowflake Iceberg Tables allow you to store data in your own S3/GCS/Azure Blob bucket in Iceberg format while querying it with Snowflake's compute engine — you own the data, not Snowflake. There are two modes: (1) Snowflake-managed: Snowflake writes data in Iceberg format to your object storage, managing the metadata. You get Snowflake's full DML support. (2) External Iceberg tables: Snowflake reads existing Iceberg tables written by other engines (Flink, Spark) — read-only by default, with periodic catalog refresh (`REFRESH` command or auto-refresh via event notifications). The integration uses Snowflake's external volume (pointing to your S3 bucket) and integrates with REST catalogs (Snowflake acts as an Iceberg catalog consumer). Key benefit: compute portability — write with Flink/Spark, query with Snowflake, Trino, or Spark without data duplication. Key limitation: Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables currently don't support all Snowflake features (clustering, search optimization). This is the architecture enabling 'bring your own compute' to the lakehouse.
